# Security Policy

Thank you for helping keep Coral secure.

## Reporting a Vulnerability

If you believe you have discovered a security vulnerability in Coral, **please do not open a public GitHub issue**.

Instead, report it privately by contacting the maintainers through one of the following methods:

- Open a private security advisory on GitHub (preferred, if available).
- Contact us through our [Discord](https://discord.gg/B2BgjwDX8m) server and request to speak with a maintainer privately.

Please include as much information as possible, such as:

- A description of the vulnerability.
- Steps to reproduce the issue.
- The affected version or commit hash.
- Any proof-of-concept code or screenshots (if applicable).
- Any suggested mitigation or fix.

## What to Expect

After receiving your report, we will:

- Acknowledge receipt of the report as soon as reasonably possible.
- Investigate and verify the vulnerability.
- Work on a fix if the issue is confirmed.
- Coordinate responsible disclosure when appropriate.

We appreciate responsible disclosure and ask that you do not publicly disclose a vulnerability until a fix has been released or we've agreed on a disclosure timeline.

## Supported Versions

Coral is currently in active development. Security fixes are generally applied to the latest development branch, and older commits may not receive patches.

## Scope

This policy applies to the Coral source code and its official releases. Issues caused by third-party software, operating systems, or unsupported modifications may fall outside the scope of this policy.

Thank you for helping make Coral safer for everyone.
